query: Xiqu Centre Tea House Theatre Experience September 2026 official Hong Kong West Kowloon

results.title: TEA HOUSE THEATRE EXPERIENCE
results.content:
TEA HOUSE THEATRE EXPERIENCE Xiqu Centre, West Kowloon Cultural District TEA HOUSE THEATRE The Tea House Theatre is a performance space designed to echo the environment of early 20th century tea houses. With table and chair seating, it offers audiences an authentic way to experience Chinese opera (xiqu), and to learn about this heritage art while enjoying tea and dim sum. [...] The Tea House Theatre Experience is our flagship introduction to Cantonese opera, the xiqu genre popular in Hong Kong, Guangdong and Macau. Presented by the talented young artists of the Xiqu Centre’s Tea House Rising Stars Troupe under the guidance of renowned Cantonese opera artist and Artistic Curator and Director Law Ka-ying, the programme presents a selection of excerpts illustrating a range of vocal, musical and performance styles.
“With our expert moderator guiding the audience through the show, the 90-minute programme offers a glimpse into Cantonese opera that everyone can enjoy,” says producer Naomi Chung.
In addition, the Tea House Theatre also hosts a variety of traditional and experimental productions by visiting troupes showcasing xiqu genres from around the region. [...] The first performance is a nanyin piece about the unique features of the Xiqu Centre and the Tea House Theatre written by renowned Cantonese opera artist Wan Yok-yu. Nanyin (naamyam in Cantonese) is a form of musical storytelling or narrative ballad singing that combines spoken words with instrumental accompaniment. It is also a singing style that has been integrated into Cantonese opera. Popular in the Pearl River Delta since the early 20th century, nanyin was historically performed by blind artists in tea houses, bars and sing-song halls. Singing about the lives of ordinary people, artists would be accompanied by musicians playing zither and yehu (a two-stringed instrument with a soundbox made from a coconut shell), and using clappers to mark out the beat. results.raw_content:
TEA HOUSE THEATRE EXPERIENCE Xiqu Centre, West Kowloon Cultural District TEA HOUSE THEATRE The Tea House Theatre is a performance space designed to echo the environment of early 20th century tea houses. With table and chair seating, it offers audiences an authentic way to experience Chinese opera (xiqu), and to learn about this heritage art while enjoying tea and dim sum.
The Tea House Theatre Experience is our flagship introduction to Cantonese opera, the xiqu genre popular in Hong Kong, Guangdong and Macau. Presented by the talented young artists of the Xiqu Centre’s Tea House Rising Stars Troupe under the guidance of renowned Cantonese opera artist and Artistic Curator and Director Law Ka-ying, the programme presents a selection of excerpts illustrating a range of vocal, musical and performance styles.
“With our expert moderator guiding the audience through the show, the 90-minute programme offers a glimpse into Cantonese opera that everyone can enjoy,” says producer Naomi Chung.
In addition, the Tea House Theatre also hosts a variety of traditional and experimental productions by visiting troupes showcasing xiqu genres from around the region.
Programme PROGRAMME I Nanyin II Paiziqu Wind and Percussion Music Wind in the Pines Wind and Percussion Sung Piece Pomegranate Blossom / Melancholy *Performance includes one sung piece III Cantonese Opera Excerpt “Meeting in a Dream” from Goddess of the Luo River IV Music Ensemble Performance Birds Returning to the Forest Dabbling in the Water V Cantonese Opera Song Romance of the Phoenix Tower / “Memorial Speech” from The Moon Pavilion / Seeking a Beauty / Strengthening Resolve / Vermilion Phoenix / Lady Zhaojun Going Beyond the Great Wall / Dream Reunion / Wandering the Lakes *Performance includes one song VI Cantonese Opera Excerpt “A Duel of Immortal Snakes” from The Legend of the White Snake INTERMISSION for Fridays NANYIN The first performance is a nanyin piece about the unique features of the Xiqu Centre and the Tea House Theatre written by renowned Cantonese opera artist Wan Yok-yu. Nanyin (naamyam in Cantonese) is a form of musical storytelling or narrative ballad singing that combines spoken words with instrumental accompaniment. It is also a singing style that has been integrated into Cantonese opera. Popular in the Pearl River Delta since the early 20th century, nanyin was historically performed by blind artists in tea houses, bars and sing-song halls. Singing about the lives of ordinary people, artists would be accompanied by musicians playing zither and yehu (a two-stringed instrument with a soundbox made from a coconut shell), and using clappers to mark out the beat. A large number of traditional nanyin songs are still performed today.
Paiziqu PAIZIQU Paiziqu is a type of fixed melody that can be sung with different lyrics or performed on wind and percussion instruments such as suona (double-reeded horn) and drums. Popular paiziqu can appear across different productions, or be strung together to create whole sequences.
Cantonese Opera Excerpts Wind and Percussion Music Performance Wind in the Pines Commonly performed in pre-battle scenes, the tight rhythm of this tune depicts the high spirits of the troops before they are sent to action.
Wind and Percussion Sung Piece Pomegranate Blossom Familiar from The Imperial Emperor of Heaven Holding Court, a traditional set piece staged before festival performances, this song describes a heavenly decree to save mortals from suffering.
Wind and Percussion Sung Piece Melancholy Professing her sorrow, Wang Zhaojun, one of the Four Beauties of Ancient China, leaves her homeland for a political marriage agreed by the Emperor.
CANTONESE OPERA EXCERPTS Cantonese opera is marked by a minimalist aesthetic that relies on stagecraft and symbolism rather than props. Performances take place on a pared-down stage, and performers, whose faces are painted with exaggerated expressions to convey heightened emotions and character type, tell the story through their gestures and mastery of the four skills fundamental to Chinese opera – singing, acting, recitation and martial arts. The excerpts you will see showcase exciting and technically demanding parts of well-known works that demonstrate particular skills and techniques.
“Meeting in a Dream” from Goddess of the Luo River Before his death, the powerful warlord Cao Cao arranges for his scholarly son Zhi to become his successor and marry the daughter of a government official. Zhi, however, is deeply in love with Zhen Fu, a woman his brother Pi also covets. When Cao arranges a marriage between Zhen Fu and Pi, a complicated power struggle begins to play out and Zhen Fu drowns herself in despair. One night, Zhi encounters Zhen Fu in a dream, transformed into the Goddess of the Luo River. Written in the 1980s by playwright Yip Siu-tak, the work is partly based on the poetry of Cao’s son Zhi.
“A Duel of Immortal Snakes” from The Legend of the White Snake Written by emerging playwright Lee Ting-fung, this martial arts piece is a new adaptation of a popular segment from The Legend of the White Snake. With choreography by Peking opera performer Han Yanming, it features stylised northern gestures and elements such as the double sword fight.
Leaving the celestial realm in search of her banished lover, Lady White Snake, Bai Suzhen, encounters a green snake spirit. After she rejects his advances a battle ensues and the green snake is defeated. Taking on female form, the green snake pledges to accompany Bai as a loyal servant on her travels.
Music Ensemble Performance MUSIC ENSEMBLE PERFORMANCE Birds Returning to the Forest Composed by Cantonese music virtuoso Yi Jianquan, this piece uses the high-pitched two-stringed gaohu to conjure up the lively chirping of birds as they return to roost at dusk.
Dabbling in the Water Also known as Heroes on Pacing Horses, this brisk, lively piece evokes the sure-footed high spirits of horses and riders galloping through wet grasslands. Cantonese Opera Song CANTONESE OPERA SONG Romance of the Phoenix Tower Written for a production that premiered in 1962, this song describes a Song-dynasty princess’s tearful farewell to her lover, a general tasked with escorting her to her homeland. “Memorial Speech” from The Moon Pavilion Composed in 1958 by acclaimed playwright Tong Tik-sang, this poignant eulogy depicts a man mourning the death of his beloved wife. Seeking a Beauty First performed in the late 1930s, this melancholy song tells of a minister’s anxious attempt to devise a honey trap to rescue his king from enemy hands. Strengthening Resolve Defeated, captured, and forced to serve the Wu King, Goujian the King of Yue withstands the humiliation by plotting a perfect revenge. This short song showcases an array of different Cantonese opera music styles.
Vermilion Phoenix Written for the 1957 Cantonese opera of the same name, this aria sung by the heroine Liu Hongluan describes how troubled life has deepened her understanding of love.
Lady Zhaojun Going Beyond the Great Wall To help her country avoid a diplomatic disaster, Wang Zhaojun accepts a political marriage in a foreign state. Before leaving her homeland, she sings a song of respect for the Emperor. Dream Reunion Written in the 1960s, and based on a chapter from the classic novel Dream of the Red Chamber, this song depicts Jia Baoyu mourning the untimely death of his lover Lin Daiyu and being visited by her spirit. Wandering the Lakes After successfully restoring their kingdom through a honey-trap scheme, minister Fan Li and concubine Xi Shi decide to withdraw from politics in a struggle between state and personal loyalties.
Artistic Team ARTISTIC TEAM Production Team Law Ka-ying Naomi Chung Cheng Wing-mui Han Yanming Janet Wong Lee Tik-lun Mai Jiawei Szeto Wai-suet John Ho Chun-hei Yue Kin-long Yuen Ka-yi Liu Chun-hei Wong Hai-wing Man Shuet-kau Christie To Wing-sum Chan Kei-ting Chan Hei-yu Mo Huamin Wong Ho-yau Vong Seng-pan Xiaoyu King Fa-lui Alan Tam Wing-lun ARTISTIC CURATOR AND DIRECTOR PRODUCER REHEARSAL CURATOR ACTION CHOREOGRAPHER MUSIC ARRANGER ENSEMBLE LEADER PERCUSSION ENSEMBLE LEADER MUSICIANS PERFORMERS PRODUCTION TEAM Au Yick-ho Rae Wu Katrina Chan Leung Nga-chi Chan Chi-nok Lai Kam-shan Chak Wing-kiu Mak Hoi-yan Barry Ling Fong Ka-yin Leo Siu Dan Fong Kitty Ho Vikki Weston Cindy Chan Loretta Tsang Penelope Zhou Nathan Woolley Zhu Yayun Nick Chan Vanessa Yu Sherlock Lam Your feedback is important to us Please fill in the audience survey EXECUTIVE PRODUCER TECHNICAL DIRECTOR PRODUCTION MANAGER STAGE MANAGER DEPUTY STAGE MANAGERS PRODUCER’S ASSISTANT LIGHTING DESIGNER VIDEO DESIGNER EDITORS ENGLISH TRANSLATORS MARKETING AND PROMOTION

The Tea House Theatre Experience is designed to introduce new audiences to Cantonese opera. It is a 90-minute showcase selection of songs, instrumental musical pieces, and performance excerpts that demonstrate a range of traditional styles through a varying programme of works exploring tales of romance, comedy, tragedy, and martial arts. Audiences are served with fine tea and dim sum during the performance - a delicious way to learn about the heritage of Chinese traditional theatre.

As the exclusive Travel Partner of West Kowloon Cultural District, we enrich your journey in Hong Kong with this special offer to explore Chinese opera and culture. # 

## **Enjoy 10% off on the Tea House Theatre Experience**

Book your flights online to enjoy discount on Tea House Theatre Experience at Xiqu Centre, West Kowloon Cultural District.

### Specially curated selection of Chinese opera performances

The Tea House Theatre Experience is designed to introduce new audiences to Cantonese opera. It is a 90-minute showcase selection of songs, instrumental musical pieces, and performance excerpts that demonstrate a range of traditional styles through a varying programme of works exploring tales of romance, comedy, tragedy, and martial arts. Audiences are served with fine tea and dim sum during the performance - a delicious way to learn about the heritage of Chinese traditional theatre.

As the exclusive Travel Partner of West Kowloon Cultural District, we enrich your journey in Hong Kong with this special offer to explore Chinese opera and culture.

#### 1. Book now

Book your flight to Hong Kong at [www.cathaypacific.com](/content/cx/en_HK.html) and we’ll send you a booking confirmation email.

#### 2. Redeem your code

On the booking confirmation page/email, click on the online booking bonus offers promotional banner and you’ll be directed to the page to redeem the promo code.

#### 3. Purchase The Tea House Theatre ticket

Select schedule and seats, followed by choosing the ticket type "Promotion Code" and enter the promo code. Ensure 10% discount applied before checkout.

#### 4. Visit The Tea House Theatre

Download the e-ticket in the confirmation email and present it at the entrance of Tea House theatre, Xiqu Centre for admission.

### Introduce new audiences to Cantonese opera

Presented by Tea House Rising Stars Troupe and curated and directed by legendary Cantonese opera artist Law Ka-ying, the performance is supplemented with narration by an expert moderator offering insights into the history of the art form.

results.title: Art March Hong Kong 2026
results.content:
Presented by WestK Performing Arts, The Tea House Theatre Experience is curated and directed by renowned Cantonese opera master Law Ka-ying and presented by WestK’s very own Tea House Rising Stars Troupe.   
   
 The Tea House Theatre Experience is specially designed to introduce new audiences to Cantonese opera. During the 90-mintute showcase, audience can enjoy a selection of Cantonese opera excerpts, songs and instrumental music complete with traditional tea and dim sum in a theatre designed with elements inspired by traditional Chinese tea houses. [...] 「粵・樂・茶韻」由西九文化區管理局表演藝術主管（戲曲）鍾珍珍監製、茶館新星劇團演出，並由著名粵劇紅伶羅家英擔任藝術策劃及導演，為觀眾打造獨特的戲曲入門體驗。  
   
 精心策劃的90分鐘節目，包括精選折子戲、粵曲演唱和粵樂演奏，輔以深入淺出的導賞解說。欣賞表演之餘，觀眾還可在布局參考傳統中式茶館的劇場中品嘗香茶點心。  
   
 新一季除繼續上演羅家英的首本戲外，更加入膾炙人口的唐滌生名作《帝女花》之〈庵遇〉片段與《紫釵記》之〈劍合釵圓〉，以及有關女中豪傑女將樊梨花的新排劇目《烽火結奇緣》。  
   
 本節目由西九演藝專屬鐘錶合作夥伴勞力士所支持。

"「粤・乐・茶韵」" 由西九文化区管理局表演艺术主管（戏曲）钟珍珍监制、茶馆新星剧团演出，并由著名粤剧红伶罗家英担任艺术策划及导演，为观众打造独特的戏曲入门体验。  
   
 精心策划的90分钟节目，包括精选折子戏、粤曲演唱和粤乐演奏，辅以深入浅出的导赏解说。欣赏表演之余，观众还可在布局参考传统中式茶馆的剧场中品尝香茶点心。  
   
 新一季除继续上演罗家英的首本戏外，更加入脍炙人口的唐涤生名作《帝女花》之〈庵遇〉片段与《紫钗记》之〈剑合钗圆〉，以及有关女中豪杰女将樊梨花的新排剧目《烽火结奇缘》。  
   
 本节目由西九演艺专属钟表合作伙伴劳力士所支持。 "

West Kolwoon Cultural District
Logo [...] The upcoming season includes two popular excerpts by renowned librettist Tong Tik-sang – “Reunion at the Nunnery” from Princess Cheung Ping and “Reunion of Sword and Hairpin” from The Story of the Purple Hairpin – as well as the martial arts piece Fan Lihua Battles Wang Yinglong.  
   
 The programme is supported by Rolex, the Exclusive Timepiece for WestK Performing Arts.

Event Details|活動詳情|活动详情

Presented by WestK Performing Arts, The Tea House Theatre Experience is curated and directed by renowned Cantonese opera master Law Ka-ying and presented by WestK’s very own Tea House Rising Stars Troupe.   
   
 The Tea House Theatre Experience is specially designed to introduce new audiences to Cantonese opera. During the 90-mintute showcase, audience can enjoy a selection of Cantonese opera excerpts, songs and instrumental music complete with traditional tea and dim sum in a theatre designed with elements inspired by traditional Chinese tea houses.   
   
 The upcoming season includes two popular excerpts by renowned librettist Tong Tik-sang – “Reunion at the Nunnery” from Princess Cheung Ping and “Reunion of Sword and Hairpin” from The Story of the Purple Hairpin – as well as the martial arts piece Fan Lihua Battles Wang Yinglong.  
   
 The programme is supported by Rolex, the Exclusive Timepiece for WestK Performing Arts.

「粵・樂・茶韻」由西九文化區管理局表演藝術主管（戲曲）鍾珍珍監製、茶館新星劇團演出，並由著名粵劇紅伶羅家英擔任藝術策劃及導演，為觀眾打造獨特的戲曲入門體驗。  
   
 精心策劃的90分鐘節目，包括精選折子戲、粵曲演唱和粵樂演奏，輔以深入淺出的導賞解說。欣賞表演之餘，觀眾還可在布局參考傳統中式茶館的劇場中品嘗香茶點心。  
   
 新一季除繼續上演羅家英的首本戲外，更加入膾炙人口的唐滌生名作《帝女花》之〈庵遇〉片段與《紫釵記》之〈劍合釵圓〉，以及有關女中豪傑女將樊梨花的新排劇目《烽火結奇緣》。  
   
 本節目由西九演藝專屬鐘錶合作夥伴勞力士所支持。

"「粤・乐・茶韵」" 由西九文化区管理局表演艺术主管（戏曲）钟珍珍监制、茶馆新星剧团演出，并由著名粤剧红伶罗家英担任艺术策划及导演，为观众打造独特的戏曲入门体验。  
   
 精心策划的90分钟节目，包括精选折子戏、粤曲演唱和粤乐演奏，辅以深入浅出的导赏解说。欣赏表演之余，观众还可在布局参考传统中式茶馆的剧场中品尝香茶点心。  
   
 新一季除继续上演罗家英的首本戏外，更加入脍炙人口的唐涤生名作《帝女花》之〈庵遇〉片段与《紫钗记》之〈剑合钗圆〉，以及有关女中豪杰女将樊梨花的新排剧目《烽火结奇缘》。  
   
 本节目由西九演艺专属钟表合作伙伴劳力士所支持。 "

results.title: Tea House Theatre Experience
results.content:
Catharina Cheung

### Time Out says

The Sunbeam Theatre, one of Hong Kong’s last bastions of Cantonese opera, has recently closed down, but at least there’s still the Xiqu Centre to carry the torch of Chinese operatic shows. Curated and directed by the legendary Cantonese opera artist Law Ka-ying, the Tea House Theatre Experience programme is designed for newcomers to Cantonese opera, featuring show excerpts and songs ranging from romance to martial arts. Highlights of the season include ‘Rain-soaked Clothes’ from A Mirror Seal, ‘Love and Struggle’ from The Warrior’s Marriage, and operatic performances in archaic Mandarin. Audiences will also be served traditional tea and dim sum during the performances, a hark back to Hong Kong’s early 20th-–century tea houses. [...] #### Choose a Time Out City

#### Choose a Time Out Market

![Xiqu Centre’s Tea House Theatre Experience](https://media.timeout.com/images/105843800/750/422/image.jpg "Xiqu Centre’s Tea House Theatre Experience")

# Tea House Theatre Experience

![Catharina Cheung](https://media.timeout.com/images/106034429/170/170/image.jpg)

### Time Out says

The [Sunbeam Theatre](https://www.timeout.com/hong-kong/news/a-look-back-at-sunbeam-theatre-its-history-and-cultural-significance-022525), one of Hong Kong’s last bastions of Cantonese opera, has recently closed down, but at least there’s still the Xiqu Centre to carry the torch of Chinese operatic shows. Curated and directed by the legendary Cantonese opera artist Law Ka-ying, the Tea House Theatre Experience programme is designed for newcomers to Cantonese opera, featuring show excerpts and songs ranging from romance to martial arts. Highlights of the season include ‘Rain-soaked Clothes’ from *A Mirror Seal*, ‘Love and Struggle’ from *The Warrior’s Marriage*, and operatic performances in archaic Mandarin. Audiences will also be served traditional tea and dim sum during the performances, a hark back to Hong Kong’s early 20th-–century tea houses.

[Tickets](https://www.westk.hk/en/events/teahouse?tab=overview) run from $300 to $342, inclusive of food and drink.

### Details

Been there, done that? Think again, my friend.

results.title: Xiqu Centre
results.content:
For an unforgettable experience, groups can purchase tickets for shows staged in Tea House Theatre and enjoy complimentary fine tea and dim sum while watching a traditional opera performance in an authentic setting.

Tea House Theatre Experience

TheTea House Theatre is an intimate 168-seat venue with a style evoking traditional Chinese tea houses. Its smaller scale, flexible seating and stall and balcony areas make it perfect for accessible traditional performances and black box productions. [...] >

INSPIRING MICE IDEAS

>

Xiqu Centre

# Xiqu Centre

Arts and Culture Culture & Heritage Unique Venues West Kowloon

Learn about the past in the present through Cantonese opera and Chinese theatre in the modern and contemporary setting of Xiqu Centre.

Xiqu Centre is a unique space in the West Kowloon Cultural District to enjoy award-winning Chinese operas. With elaborate architectural design inspired by a soft lantern glowing behind a beaded curtain, the venue combines the past with the present to celebrate Cantonese opera and Chinese theatre in a contemporary setting. Apart from the 1,075- seat Grand Theatre and the 168-seat Tea House Theatre, Xiqu Centre also features FHKA Seminar Hall and 8 rehearsal studios. [...] The Xiqu Centre’s signature introduction to Cantonese opera offers a regular programme of excerpts, music and songs. Staged in the Tea House Theatre by the resident Rising Stars Tea House Troupe, the 90-minute performance is narrated by an expert moderator and subtitled in Chinese and English. Perfect for a unique corporate cultural event, the Tea House Theatre Experience takes you back in time for an up-close taste of Cantonese opera the heritage way.

Grand Theatre

A world-class venue showcasing high-quality traditional and contemporary xiqu performances by artists from Hong Kong, Chinese Mainland and the region. Tailor-made for professional xiqu presentations and fitted with state-of-the-art lighting and sound equipment, the theatre has a seating capacity of 1,075.

# Xiqu Centre

[Arts and Culture](/eng/home/mice-ideas.html?tag=arts) [Culture & Heritage](/eng/home/mice-ideas.html?tag=culture-and-heritage) [Unique Venues](/eng/home/mice-ideas.html?tag=venues) [West Kowloon](/eng/home/mice-ideas.html?tag=west-kowloon)

![](/.imaging/image-1000x1000/dam/964de835-f682-4eaa-ae12-2e733d6fdf56/Xiqu%20Carousel%201.webp)

![](/.imaging/image-1000x1000/dam/632e17ac-c964-4a8f-9736-4e2eff3daef6/Xiqu%20Carousel%202.webp)

![](/.imaging/image-1000x1000/dam/1743552b-325b-4aa1-a5f9-b4cae3b22654/Xiqu%20Carousel%203.webp)

![](/.imaging/image-1000x1000/dam/30c10b4e-e5c8-4a3a-aba0-4da3e6c01ce2/Xiqu%20Carousel%204.webp)

![](/.imaging/image-1000x1000/dam/f947e0ba-e0a2-448f-b6c0-fefb35500e1d/Xiqu%20Carousel%205.webp)

Learn about the past in the present through Cantonese opera and Chinese theatre in the modern and contemporary setting of Xiqu Centre.

Xiqu Centre is a unique space in the West Kowloon Cultural District to enjoy award-winning Chinese operas. With elaborate architectural design inspired by a soft lantern glowing behind a beaded curtain, the venue combines the past with the present to celebrate Cantonese opera and Chinese theatre in a contemporary setting. Apart from the 1,075- seat Grand Theatre and the 168-seat Tea House Theatre, Xiqu Centre also features FHKA Seminar Hall and 8 rehearsal studios.

For an unforgettable experience, groups can purchase tickets for shows staged in Tea House Theatre and enjoy complimentary fine tea and dim sum while watching a traditional opera performance in an authentic setting.

![](/.imaging/image-700x400/dam/91f092f9-0785-41e1-a7ae-c18c6f408c72/Xiqu-tea-house.webp)

![](/.imaging/image-700x400/dam/3f2b608d-5599-41c5-826c-9e8a97d5d4e7/Xiqu-tea-house-02.webp)

**Tea House Theatre Experience**

The*Tea House Theatre* is an intimate 168-seat venue with a style evoking traditional Chinese tea houses. Its smaller scale, flexible seating and stall and balcony areas make it perfect for accessible traditional performances and black box productions.

The Xiqu Centre’s signature introduction to Cantonese opera offers a regular programme of excerpts, music and songs. Staged in the Tea House Theatre by the resident Rising Stars Tea House Troupe, the 90-minute performance is narrated by an expert moderator and subtitled in Chinese and English. Perfect for a unique corporate cultural event, the *Tea House Theatre Experience* takes you back in time for an up-close taste of Cantonese opera the heritage way.

![](/.imaging/image-700x400/dam/fef30b88-9437-4dfc-b641-d3a439c2c08e/Xiqu%20Fig.1.webp)

**Grand Theatre**

A world-class venue showcasing high-quality traditional and contemporary xiqu performances by artists from Hong Kong, Chinese Mainland and the region. Tailor-made for professional xiqu presentations and fitted with state-of-the-art lighting and sound equipment, the theatre has a seating capacity of 1,075.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Capacity** | **Usable Area** |
| **Grand Theatre** | 1,075 pax | Fixed Seats |
| **Tea House Theatre** | 168 pax | Fixed seats |
| **FHKA Seminar Hall** | 112 pax | Fixed seats |
| **Studios** | Varying from 37-167 pax | Varying from 40-250 sq. m. |

![Hong Kong Cheapo logo](https://cdn.cheapoguides.com/wp-content/themes/cheapo_theme/assets/img/logos/hongkongcheapo/logo.svg.gzip "Hong Kong Cheapo")
![WestK Cabaret Festival](https://cdn.cheapoguides.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/7/2025/08/cropped-WestK-Cabaret-Festival-2026-7-1024x600.jpg)

# WestK Cabaret Festival

## Add to Calendar

![](https://cdn.cheapoguides.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/7/2025/10/Holly-Booth-no-background-e1759735686243-141x150.png)

Get ready for Hong Kong’s first cabaret festival featuring music, comedy, storytelling, theatrical flair and more.

## What to expect

![](https://cdn.cheapoguides.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/7/2025/08/WestK-Cabaret-Festival-2026-649x1024.jpg)

For one week, the [Xiqu Centre](https://hongkongcheapo.com/place/xiqu-centre/) at [West Kowloon Cultural District](https://hongkongcheapo.com/place/west-kowloon-art-park/) will be transformed into a vibrant hub, hosting a wide range of cross-cultural entertainment.

The line-up includes Broadway star Patti LuPone, French cabaret from Paradis Latin, Bobby Fox’s *Mr Entertainment*, Alex Lam’s Cantopop cabaret, drag comedy, Spanish queer cabaret, immersive studio performances, documentary screenings and dancefloor sessions. There will also be free drop-in performances at Happy Hour @ Atrium, with local acts covering circus, a cappella, swing dance, musical theatre, tap, folk dance and hip-hop. No registration is needed for the Atrium performances.

## Highlights

There’s a bunch of performances, but here’s a few we’re especially looking forward to:

Tickets go on sale at 10 a.m. on April 9, 2026. Patti LuPone and Alex Lam tickets are available via Cityline and Art-mate; *Big Brown Girl* tickets are available via Art-mate.

## Add to Calendar

## Tokyo's Best Events and Cultural Activities in Your Inbox Every Week

### Expand Your Horizons with MICE

Looking for inspiring meetings, conferences, or exhibitions? Explore MICE events tailored to your interests.

[Click Here to Learn More >](https://www.mehongkong.com/eng/home/planning/events.html)

**Disclaimer:**

This page highlights Hong Kong’s year-round events for public information.

Some events listed on this page are organised by the Hong Kong Tourism Board (HKTB). For events organised independently by third-party organisers, in which HKTB does not involve, and third-party events supported or promoted by HKTB, it is the sole responsibility of the respective organisers to ensure smooth delivery of these events.

The information, images and links presented on this page reflect details available at the time of listing and are provided for general reference only. For the latest status and details of events, please refer to the announcements issued by the relevant organisers.

HKTB provides no guarantee regarding the accuracy, completeness or timeliness of third-party content and accepts no responsibility or liability for any loss, damage or injury arising from participation in or attendance at any third-party events, or from reliance on the information provided on this page.
